Tonic • Postpartum recovery • Anti-inflammatory

Golden Milk

A warm, soothing turmeric milk — calms inflammation and grounds you as you heal.
Makes 1 mug
Time 5 min
Caffeine none
For you · a calm daily cup
When to drink Daytime — with or after a meal. The ghee + black pepper help your body absorb the turmeric.

Why this heals you

  • Turmeric — calms the inflammation of healing tradition
  • Black pepper + ghee — the keys that unlock the turmeric verified ×2
  • Ginger — warming; eases digestion tradition
  • Caffeine-free — calm warmth, gentle any time of day tradition
Ingredients
1 cupMilk of choice
½ tspTurmeric
½ tspGinger (or a thumb, grated)
⅛ tspBlack pepper (a good pinch)
pinchCeylon cinnamon
1 tspGhee (the absorption fat)
Method
1
Warm the milk

Gently warm the milk in a small pot — not boiling.

2
Add the spices

Whisk in the turmeric, ginger, cinnamon, ⅛ tsp black pepper and the ghee.

3
Simmer & sip

Simmer 2–3 min, pour into a mug. A little honey if you like. Sip warm.

Just for you, Mum

A calm, caffeine-free cup to reach for instead of a second coffee — warming, grounding, gentle on a depleted system.

Good to know
BestDaytime, with a meal — not too close to bedtime.
RicherA splash of coconut milk or a little MCT oil.
Why we say this
Turmeric’s anti-inflammatory action and the piperine + fat absorption boost are corroborated by ≥2 dated sources in the Knowledge Bank; the warming framing is honestly labelled tradition.

Food, not medical advice · non-diagnostic · made for this season of motherhood
